outdated_reason: Six months of training drift since calibration. Pete was significantly fitter at the time. Use as starting reference; treat zone-mapping answers as provisional until refreshed.
framework_anchor: RPE 10 = 20-min FTP effort. Scale extends to 12+ on the hardest zones.
power_notation: Bracketed value = on-paper technical scale per the chart. Value next to brackets = Pete's real-world adjusted number.

zones.name: Competent
zones.feel: Almost no physical effort, more mental concentration.
zones.use_cases: Technique drills, form work, skills sessions. Not an effort-level — a focus-level.

zones.name: Chilled
zones.feel: Super easy, should feel better after completed than before. Can hold a conversation throughout if needed.
zones.use_cases: Recovery rides, recovery jogs, recovery swims. Cooldown after a hard session. Day-after-hard-day movement.

zones.name: Comfortable
zones.feel: Should feel like you could hold this effort almost indefinitely but with some level of concentration. Should be able to still hold a conversation.
zones.use_cases: Long aerobic base building. Z2 / aerobic work. Full Ironman pace when peaking.

zones.name: Controlled
zones.feel: This should be the effort you could hold for the duration of the relevant Ironman leg when peaking. Should feel some difficulty but you are in full control of the effort. Should be sweating and breathing rate starting to increase. Holding a conversation would be difficult.
zones.use_cases: Steady-state efforts. Long tempo work. Ironman race pace.

zones.name: Challenging
zones.feel: This should be the effort you could hold for the duration of the relevant Ironman 70.3 leg when peaking. Should feel challenging to hold. Should start to feel difficult to say more than a sentence or two at a time.
zones.use_cases: Tempo work. 70.3 race pace. Half-Ironman build.

zones.name: Candy
zones.feel: Difficult to hold. Should have to concentrate & focus to maintain this effort. Close to the limit of what you can sustain for an extended period of time (20-60 minutes). Should feel difficult to say more than a few words at a time.
zones.use_cases: Sweet-spot intervals. The zone between tempo and threshold where you build aerobic ceiling without the fatigue cost of threshold work.

zones.name: Critical
zones.feel: Extremely difficult both physically and mentally to sustain for more than a few minutes. Breathing should feel very heavy and very difficult to talk. Likely to feel unsure if you could sustain this effort for more than 5 minutes.
zones.use_cases: Threshold intervals (FTP-style). 20-min FTP test efforts. The benchmark zone for the RPE 10 anchor.

zones.name: Crazy
zones.definition: Maximal effort for the interval duration
zones.feel: Should feel like everything you've got for the interval duration. May get a burning sensation in the legs during. May not feel aerobically challenged until the later part of the interval. Likely to feel heavy breathing AFTER the interval.
zones.use_cases: VO2 max intervals, sprint work, neuromuscular efforts. Short hard bursts.
